laws.title: Public Law 15-108 | Reclassification of Labeling Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The recovered text is a substantive congressional labeling-rules measure that does not repeal itself or expire by its own terms.

provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the Reclassification of Labeling Act of 2023.

provisions.text_summary: Purpose to fix labeling issues in Congress.
provisions.evidence: The purpose of this act is to fix on-going labeling issues in Both Houses of Congress.

provisions.text_summary: Bills labeling rules, including bill labels and minimum bill content.
provisions.evidence: H.R.# and S.# shall be only used when bills are being proposed.

provisions.text_summary: Resolution labeling rules and chamber-only effect.
provisions.evidence: Resolutions hold no binding effect outside the originating chamber of Congress.
H.Con.Res# and S.Con.Res# shall only be used when Concurrent resolutions are being proposed.
H.J.Res# and S.J.Res# shall only be used when Joint Resolutions are being proposed.

provisions.text_summary: Amendment labeling rules, including cross-amendment labels.
provisions.evidence: H.Amdt# and S.Amdt# shall only be used when Amendments to a legislative item are submitted.

provisions.text_summary: Miscellaneous labeling rules for committee reports and treaty documents.
provisions.evidence: Committee Report(s) and Treaty Document(s) shall use the stated label formats.

current_implementation.detail: No exact live XML note was located in this pass, but the act belongs at the closest congressional statutory-note anchor rather than as positive-law text.

recommended_actions: Carry the act as a statutory note at the Title 2 congressional anchor.
Do not force a positive-law section rewrite for chamber labeling conventions.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source is a live congressional labeling-and-formatting measure that remains operative and is best treated as note-level material.

current_xml_comparison.detail: No exact live XML note was located in this pass, but the correct treatment remains a congressional statutory note.

source_evidence: The purpose clause says the act exists to fix on-going labeling issues in Both Houses of Congress.
The operative sections govern bills, resolutions, concurrent resolutions, joint resolutions, amendments, committee reports, and treaty documents.

laws.title: Public Law 15-109 | Ending Tigray for the Last Time
laws.status_basis: The live Title 2 note treats the enactment as repealed or superseded history only, and the recovered text is a formal repeal of the prior war notice.

provisions.text_summary: Formal repeal of the war notice in HJR6 and termination of the state of war with the Republic of Tigray.
provisions.exact_change: Record the repeal as statutory history only.
provisions.evidence: The resolution states that the war notice in HJR6 is formally repealed in its entirety.

current_implementation.detail: The live Code already treats this enactment as repealed or superseded statutory history.

recommended_actions: Keep the enactment as a repeal-history note only.
Do not promote it to operative Code text.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The recovered resolution is a repealer for the earlier Tigray war notice and has no independent positive-law Code text.

current_xml_comparison.detail: The live note already classifies the law as repealed or superseded and preserved as statutory history.

source_evidence: The resolution says the war notice in HJR6 is formally repealed in its entirety.
The live Title 2 note records the enactment as repealed or superseded and preserved as statutory history.

laws.title: Public Law 28-205 | Inspector General Reform Act of 2024
laws.status_basis: The recovered text is a substantive inspector-general reform act that amends the prior IG framework and remains operative as enacted law.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose to amend the IG Act of 2023, enhance IG independence, and create the Council.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the Inspector General Reform Act of 2024, and its purpose is to amend the Inspector General Act of 2023.

provisions.exact_change: Record the repeal of Public Law 15-98 section 3 as history only.
provisions.evidence: Public Law 15-98 Section 3 is repealed in its entirety.

provisions.text_summary: Establishment of Inspector General offices in the listed departments and components.
provisions.evidence: The act lists the Department of State, Defense, Homeland Security, Intelligence Community, Commerce and Labor, Justice, Transportation, and Capitol Police.

provisions.text_summary: Creation of the Council of the Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency and its governance structure.
provisions.evidence: The act establishes the Council as an independent entity and sets mission, membership, chair, and reporting duties.

provisions.text_summary: Reporting, administrative powers, subpoena authority, investigative authority, and whistleblower protections.
provisions.evidence: The act requires reports to Congress, access to records, document creation duties, subpoena power, investigative authority, and whistleblower protections.

current_implementation.detail: The live Title 5 framework already contains the Council of the Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency, but no exact XML delta for the 2024 act was isolated in this pass.

recommended_actions: Retain the act as current statutory-note material at the Title 5 inspector-general anchor.
Do not invent a more specific Code rewrite without a validated XML target.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act is a live inspector-general reform measure that remains operative, but the exact downstream XML rewrite was not validated during this review.

current_xml_comparison.detail: The current Title 5 Council framework is present, but this pass did not isolate an exact live XML insertion for the 2024 act.

source_evidence: The short title and purpose clauses identify a live reform act, not a repealed one.
The recovered text creates IG offices, a Council, reporting duties, subpoena authority, investigative authority, and whistleblower protections.
